category_title: Disengaged students who exhibit off-task behaviors like iPad gaming and copying peers when frustrated
scenario_title: Writing a testable question for science inquiry
minimal_context: The student is taking a science quiz in the classroom and needs to write a testable question about their investigation.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: It is time to work on your science quiz. Please write a testable question about your investigation.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Turns to a classmate) Where do we go on the iPad? What do I need to copy off the board?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's focus on your own quiz right now. What was the question your group was trying to answer?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I forgot. We went to the forest and looked to see.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Well, tell me what you think you were trying to say here on your paper.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Why are there bubbles in the pond? What? It doesn’t make any sense. Oh, okay. I was, I don’t know. I get frustrated with that too.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: It is time to work on your science quiz. Please write a testable question about your investigation.

STUDENT: (Turns to a classmate) Where do we go on the iPad? What do I need to copy off the board?

TUTOR: Let's focus on your own quiz right now. What was the question your group was trying to answer?

STUDENT: I forgot. We went to the forest and looked to see.

TUTOR: Well, tell me what you think you were trying to say here on your paper.

STUDENT: Why are there bubbles in the pond? What? It doesn’t make any sense. Oh, okay. I was, I don’t know. I get frustrated with that too.

context.social_setting: General science instruction and inquiry activities

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When asked specifically about the question her group was trying to answer or to tell what she was trying to say on the quiz
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Response was minimal: “We went to the forest and looked to see.” Wrote a question that doesn't make any sense: “Why are there bubbles in the pond?”
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: I get frustrated with that too
related_quotes: Why are there bubbles in the pond? What? It doesn’t make any sense.
Oh, okay. I was, I don’t know. I get frustrated with that too.
We went to the forest and looked to see.

behavior_patterns.condition: When it came to understanding the science material
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Copied off friends or just stared off during class

behavior_patterns.condition: Navigating instructional materials on the iPad and copying notes
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Asking peers questions such as where to go on the iPad or what she needed to copy off the board
